steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the reference to the parlor as Tía Ana's "special province" accomplishes. The passage establishes the parlor as her territory — her school — now used nightly as Salomé's salon, with Tía Ana returning each morning to check whether guests have left so the room can revert to her classroom.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'emphasize her sense of honor regarding the parlor': No mention of honor or reputation in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — 'suggest she views the parlor as a social escape': The text says nothing about Tía Ana seeking escape from social obligations. ✗
  • Choice C — 'imply she feels betrayed by the repurposing': The tone doesn't support betrayal — her eagerness is territorial, not wounded. ✗
  • Choice D — 'establish her sense of ownership over the parlor': Calling the parlor her "special province" establishes her sense of ownership, which is precisely what makes her eager for guests to leave so the room can return to being hers. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
